text.title: Index Tuning — Procedure
text.summary: Five steps: baseline → sweep M / ef_search → quantize → re-bench → ship.

step.name: baseline-bench
step.action: Run 1k+ queries. Measure recall@k vs ground truth, p50/p95 latency, memory used. Record in `tuning-history.yaml` row "baseline".
step.decision-gate: If baseline already passes SLA — STOP; tuning unnecessary (r1-bench-before-tune).

step.name: sweep-m-and-ef
step.action: Pareto sweep across M ∈ {16, 32, 64} × ef_search ∈ {32, 64, 128, 200}. Record measurement each.
step.decision-gate: If no M reaches recall_floor under latency cap — quantization needed (or rethink corpus).

step.name: quantize
step.action: Add scalar int8 first; bench. If still over memory cap, try product quantization. Binary only as last resort.
step.decision-gate: If recall drops below floor — step back one quantization level (r2-quantize-with-recall-floor).

step.name: re-bench
step.action: Rebuild index with chosen params. Re-run 1k workload. Compare to baseline + SLA.
step.decision-gate: If recall regression vs baseline OR < floor — rollback (r5-recall-regression-gate).

step.name: ship
step.action: Commit `index-tuning.yaml`. Append to `tuning-history.yaml`. Deploy via blue/green (rebuild not in-place).
step.output: Production index live with new config
step.decision-gate: If post-deploy latency drifts >20% — investigate; may indicate corpus growth (re-bench).
